"fll{)(f;l()f.ro 913 (3t)/~ ~1RT ~ 9.1~.1976 · mo(t;Jof.to 197 (3t)/~ iNWlAT ~ 29.3.1978 · mOCf)1()f.to79 (31)/~ ~ ~ 24.2.1982 1 . mo<f5TO~O695 (3t)/~ ~lAT aRRJ 9.09.1983 · moCfilOf.to 903 (3t)~ ~1AT ~ 29.11.2000 · mo(f)f()~O 113(3i)/~.CffijfFU ~ 20.2.2003 . 1 . mo(t;Jof.to 204(3i)/~ ~ ~ 22.3.2004 ~TRY OF CONSUMER AFFAIRS, ~D AND PUBLIC DISTRIBUTION (Department of r=ood and Public Dl8trtbutIon) ORDER New Dethi, the 10th November, 2006 .0. 194O(E).-In exercise of the powers conferred by Section 3 of the Essential' Comm dities Act, 1955 (10 of 1955), the central Govemment hereby makes the fOllowing Order f rther to amend,the Sugarcane (Contr~) Order, 1966, namely :-- 1. (1) This Order may be cat led the Sugarcane (Control) (Amendment)Order, 2006. (2) It shall come Into force on the date of its publication in the Official Gazette. 2. In Sugarcane (Control) Order, 1966,.after clause '6, the 'following clause shalt be Inserted,namely:- "6A. Reml.ction on setting up of two sugar factories within the radius of 15 Kms. - Notwithstandinganything contained In clause6, no new sugar factory shall beset up within the radiusof 15 Kms of any existing sugar factory or another new sugar factory in a state or two or morestates: Provided that the State' Government m~y with the prior approval of the Central Government, where It considers necessary and expedient In' public interest, notify such minimum distance higher than 15 Kms or different minimum distances not less than 15 Kms for different regions in their respectiveStates. 1 .I'G C?1P6 .,.,,_. --- ------- - - - -- - - -- 5 of 9 -- THEGAZEITEOFlNDIA: EXI'RAORDlNARY " ~ WAIn'Il-Sec. 3(ii)] , iI I \ \1 Explanation 1.- An existing sugar factory shall mean a sugar factory in operation and shall also include a sugar factory that has taken all effective steps as specifiedin Explanation4' to set up a sugar factory but excludes a sugar factory that has not carrled out its crushing operationsfor last five sugar seasons. Explanation 2.- A new sugar factory shall mean a sugar factory, which is not an existing sugar factory, but has filed the Industrial Entrepreneur Memorandum as prescribed by the Department of Industrial Policyand Promotion,Ministry of Commerceand Industry in the Central Government and has submitted a psrformance guarantee of rupees one crore to the Chief Director (Sugar), Departimentof Food and Public Distribution, Ministry of ConsumerAffairs, Fo(>dand Public Distribution for implementation of the Industrial Entrepreneur Memorandumwithin the stipulated time or extended tlrne as specified, in clause6C. Explanation 3.- The minimum distance shall be determined as measuredby the Survey of India. Explanation 4.- The effective steps shall mean the foflowing steps taken by the concerned person to implement th~ Industrial ! EntrepreneurMemorandumfor setting up of sugar factoryl- (a) (b) purchaseof required land in the name of th~ factory; placement of firm order for purchase of plant' and , ' . machinery for the factory and payment: of requisite advance or opening of irrevocable letter of credit with suppliers; (c) commencementof civil work and constructionof building for the factory; (dj sanction of requisite term loans from banks or financial institutions; (e) any other step prescribedby the Central G()vemment, in this regard through a notification. -- 6 of 9 -- 68. Requirements fQr.J~lino the Indultrlal Entrepreneur emorandum.- (1) . Befo~efltlng the Industrial Entrepreneur Memorand~.~ w~~hthe ",1 .,. \.', Central Government, .the concerned perso~, shall obtain .?! certificate from the Cane commisSioner or Director (Sugar) or ' Specified Authority of the concerned state Government tha't the distance between the site where he propOses to set.up sugar factory and adjacent existing sugar factories and. new sugar factories is not less than the minimum distance prescri~d by the Central Government or the State Government, as the case may be, and the concerned person shall file the Industrial Entrepreneur Memoran~m with the Central Govemm~nt within one month of Issue of such certificate failing which validity of the certificate shal! expire. (2) After· filing the Industrial Entrepreneur Memorandum, the concemed person shan subm~ a ~rformance guarantee of rupees one crore to Chief Director (Sugar), Department of Food " . and Public Distribution, Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food and Public Distribution within thirty days of filing the Industrial Entrepreneur Memorandum as a ,surety for implementation of the Industrial Entrepreneur Memorandum within the stipulated time or extended time as specified in clause 6C failing which Industrial Entrepreneur Memorandum shall stand dE!7recognized as fa r as provisions of this Order are concerned. 6C. Time limit to implement Industrial Entrepreneur Memorandum.- The stipulated time for taking effective steps shall be two years and commercial production shall commence within four years I with effect from the date of filing the Industrial Entrepreneur Memorandum with the central Government, failing which the Industrial Entrepreneur Memorandum shall stand de-recognized as far as provisions of this Order are concerned and the performance guarantee shall be forfeited: 7 -- 7 of 9 -- Provided that the Chief Director (Sugar), Dep~rtment of , . Food and Public Distribution, Ministry of t~nsumer Affairs, Food and Public Distribution : on the recommendation of the concerned State Government, may give extension of one year not exceedinq six months at a time, for implementing the iIndustrial Entrepreneur Memorandum and commencement of commercial production thereof. , THEGAZhlTEOFINDlA: EXTRAORDINARY [PART II-&C. 3 ii 60. Consequences of non-implementation: of the provisions laid down in clauses 68 and 6C.+- If an Industrial Entrepreneur Memorandum remains unimplemented within the time specifled in clause 6C, the performance guarant~e furnished for its implementation shall be forfeited after giving the concerned person a reasonable opportunltv of being heard. 6E. Application of clauses 68, 6C and 60 to tWeperson whose Industrial Entrepreneur Memorandum haf already been acknowledged. - (1) Except the period specified in sub-clause (2): of clause 68 of this Order, the other provisions specified in clauses 66, , 6C and 6D' shall also be applicable to the persen whose Industrial Entrepreneur Memorandum has atre~y been acknowledged as on date of this notification but whp has not taken effective steps as specifted in Explanation 4 to the clause ; 6A. (2) The person whose Industrial Entrepreneur Memorandum has already been acknowledged as on date of this notification but who has not taken effective steps as specified in Explanation 4 to the clause 6A shall furnish a performance guarantee of rupees one crore to the Chief Directlilr(Sugar), -- 8 of 9 -- I ._ ·1 [ 'WIn-lPI'1U)J 9 Department of Food and PUbli.c Distribution, Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food and Public Distribution within a period of six months of issue of this notification failing which the . Industrial Entrepreneur f!1emorandumof the concerned person shall stand de-recognized as far as provisions of this Order are concerned.". [F. No. 27(4)12006-ST] Dr. JOY I. CHEENATH. Jt.
